title: Early Valid Deassertion Violation
description: Master deasserts AWVALID before the slave asserts AWREADY, violating the AXI-Lite handshake protocol.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [AXI_PROT_CHECK] AWVALID deasserted before AWREADY high
expected_root_cause: The Write Address FSM deasserts valid on a timer rather than waiting for the ready signal from the crossbar.
failure_log:
Master: AWVALID=1, AWADDR=0x1000
[9] Master: AWVALID=0, AWADDR=0x1000
[9] Crossbar: AWREADY=0
UVM_ERROR @ 10ns: Protocol Violation: VALID dropped before READY.
success_log:
Master: AWVALID=1
[9] Crossbar: AWREADY=1
[9] Master: AWVALID=0
UVM_INFO: Handshake complete.
rtl: always_ff @(posedge clk) begin if (state == ADDR_PHASE) awvalid <= 1; if (timer == 4'hA) awvalid <= 0; end
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) begin if (state == ADDR_PHASE) awvalid <= 1; if (awvalid && awready) awvalid <= 0; end

title: Address Decoder Overlap
description: The crossbar address decoder maps the same address range to both Slave 0 and Slave 1.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_MISMATCH] Multiple slaves selected for address 0x2000
expected_root_cause: Combinational decoder logic uses overlapping bitmasks for slave selection [10].
failure_log:
AWADDR=0x2000 -> S0_SEL=1, S1_SEL=1
UVM_FATAL: Bus Contention detected.
success_log:
AWADDR=0x2000 -> S0_SEL=1, S1_SEL=0
UVM_INFO: Slave 0 selected correctly.
rtl: assign s0_sel = (addr >= 0 && addr <= 0x2FFF); assign s1_sel = (addr >= 0x2000 && addr <= 0x4000);

title: Blocking Assignment in Sequential Logic
description: Crossbar grant logic uses blocking assignments inside an always_ff block, creating a race condition [11, 12].
bug_signature: VCS-ERROR: Blocking assignment '=' used in sequential block
expected_root_cause: Grant signal is updated with '=' instead of '<=', causing simulation/synthesis mismatch [13].
failure_log: Compiler Error: Non-synthesizable blocking assignment in clocked process.
success_log: Simulation successful. Grant logic settled in 1 cycle.
rtl: always_ff @(posedge clk) begin gnt = next_gnt; end
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) begin gnt <= next_gnt; end

title: Reset State Violation (ARREADY)
description: ARREADY is high during reset, allowing the master to send addresses into an uninitialized state machine [1].
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [RST_CHECK] Interface signal ARREADY active during reset
expected_root_cause: The reset branch in the always_ff block for ARREADY is missing or sets it to 1.
failure_log:
T=5ns: rst_n=0, arready=1
UVM_ERROR: System reset protocol violation.
success_log:
T=5ns: rst_n=0, arready=0
UVM_INFO: Reset state correct.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) begin if (!rst_n) arready <= 0; else arready <= 1; end

title: Write Strobe (WSTRB) Ignored
description: The crossbar forwards all bytes regardless of the WSTRB mask, leading to data corruption in partial writes.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_COMP] Data mismatch. Expected 0x00, Got 0xFF
expected_root_cause: The W channel logic ignores the wstrb signal and performs a full word write.
failure_log:
WDATA=0xFFFFFFFF, WSTRB=0x01
Memory[0x1000] updated to 0xFFFFFFFF
UVM_ERROR: Strobe ignored.
success_log:
Memory[0x1000] updated to 0x000000FF
UVM_INFO: Partial write successful.
rtl: always_ff @(posedge clk) if (wvalid && wready) mem[addr] <= wdata;
fix_replacement: integer i; always_ff @(posedge clk) if (wvalid && wready) for(i=0; i<4; i++) if(wstrb[i]) mem[addr][(i*8)+:8] <= wdata[(i*8)+:8];

title: Unmapped Address Timeout Hang
description: The crossbar does not return a DECERR when an unmapped address is accessed, causing the master to hang indefinitely.
bug_signature: UVM_FATAL: [TIMEOUT] Simulation hang waiting for BVALID
expected_root_cause: The default slave case in the address decoder does not drive BVALID/BRESP=2'b11 [14].
failure_log:
AWADDR=0xDEADBEEF (Unmapped)
BVALID remains 0 after 1000 cycles.
success_log:
AWADDR=0xDEADBEEF -> BVALID=1, BRESP=0x3 (DECERR)
UVM_INFO: Error response received.
rtl: always_comb begin case(addr) 0x1000: sel=0; 0x2000: sel=1; endcase end
fix_replacement: always_comb begin sel=0; err=0; case(addr) 0x1000: sel=0; 0x2000: sel=1; default: err=1; endcase end

title: Read Data Mux Error
description: Read data from Slave 1 is being corrupted by Slave 0's bus due to improper OR-reduction in the crossbar [15].
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_DATA] Data 0xAAAA5555 masked by 0x00000000
expected_root_cause: Read data buses are combined with logic OR without checking which slave is active.
failure_log:
S1_RDATA=0xAAAA5555, S0_RDATA=0x00000000
Mux Result=0x00000000
UVM_ERROR: Data bus collision.
success_log:
Mux Result=0xAAAA5555
UVM_INFO: Read data integrity verified.

title: BREADY Assertion Missing
description: Master forgets to assert BREADY, causing the crossbar to hold BVALID high forever and blocking subsequent writes.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SVA_VIOLATION] BVALID high for > 100 cycles
expected_root_cause: The Master sequence/driver is not programmed to acknowledge the write response channel [16].
failure_log:
BVALID=1, BREADY=0
UVM_WARNING: Channel B blocked.
success_log:
BVALID=1, BREADY=1 -> BVALID=0
UVM_INFO: Transaction finalized.
testbench: task run(); drive_addr(); drive_data(); // Missing bready endtask

title: Incomplete Case Statement (Latch Inference)
description: Address decoding logic infers a latch because the default case is missing, leading to unstable slave selection [17, 18].
bug_signature: VCS-WARNING: Latch inferred for signal 'slave_select'
expected_root_cause: Combinational case block does not cover all possible 32-bit address values [19].
failure_log: Compiler Warning: Latch inferred for slave_select. Potential for functional glitches.
success_log: Synthesized netlist verified. No latches found.
rtl: always_comb begin case(addr[31:28]) 4'h1: sel=1; 4'h2: sel=2; endcase end
fix_replacement: always_comb begin sel=0; case(addr[31:28]) 4'h1: sel=1; 4'h2: sel=2; default: sel=0; endcase end

title: Simultaneous R/W Priority Bug
description: When ARVALID and AWVALID arrive in the same cycle, the arbiter grants both, causing internal state corruption.
bug_signature: UVM_FATAL: [ARB_CONT] Multiple grants in single cycle
expected_root_cause: The arbitration logic uses two independent 'if' statements instead of an 'if-else' priority chain [10].
failure_log:
ARVALID=1, AWVALID=1 -> READ_GNT=1, WRITE_GNT=1
UVM_ERROR: Multiple grants detected.
success_log:
AWVALID=1 -> WRITE_GNT=1, READ_GNT=0
UVM_INFO: Priority handled.
testbench: task run(); fork drive_read(); drive_write(); join endtask

title: X-Propagation in Address Phase
description: The crossbar does not check for 'X' or 'Z' values on the address bus during AWVALID, leading to unpredictable slave behavior [11].
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SVA_XZ] AWADDR contains X at posedge clk
expected_root_cause: Missing assertion in the interface or RTL to validate address integrity [20].
failure_log:
T=20: AWVALID=1, AWADDR=32'hXXXX0000
UVM_ERROR: X-Propagation detected.
success_log:
T=20: AWADDR=32'h00001000
UVM_INFO: Address is valid logic.

title: WREADY Glitch (Combinational Path)
description: WREADY depends combinationally on WVALID, creating a potential timing loop or glitch [2].
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [TIMING_LOOP] Combinational loop between Master and Slave
expected_root_cause: The slave's WREADY output logic is not registered and reacts immediately to the Master's WVALID.
failure_log:
WVALID -> WREADY -> WVALID (Loop)
Simulation terminated to prevent hang.
success_log:
WREADY asserted 1 cycle after WVALID.
UVM_INFO: Timing closure met.

title: Response (RRESP) Encoding Error
description: The crossbar returns 2'b01 (EXOKAY) instead of 2'b00 (OKAY) for a standard read, which the master does not support [21].
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_DATA] Unsupported RRESP: 0x1
expected_root_cause: Hardcoded response signal in crossbar logic is set to an incorrect AXI status code [22].
failure_log:
Read Transaction: RRESP=0x1 (EXOKAY)
UVM_ERROR: Master expects 0x0 (OKAY).
success_log:
Read Transaction: RRESP=0x0 (OKAY)
UVM_INFO: Response code valid.
testbench: task run(); `uvm_do(req) // check req.rresp in sbd endtask

title: BVALID Without Request
description: The crossbar generates a Write Response (BVALID) even though no write request was issued.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_UNEXPECTED] Received BVALID without active transaction
expected_root_cause: The B-channel state machine is triggered by a glitch or an uninitialized start signal.
failure_log:
T=100: BVALID=1 (No AW/W seen since T=0)
UVM_ERROR: Spurious response.
success_log:
BVALID only asserted after successful WVALID/WREADY.
UVM_INFO: Response sequence correct.
testbench: initial #100ns; // Wait and see if BVALID pops up

title: Address Bit Truncation
description: The address decoder only looks at bits [11:0], failing to distinguish between ranges at 0x1000 and 0x2000.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_MISMATCH] Expected Slave 1, Data written to Slave 0
expected_root_cause: The decoder slice is too narrow to cover the full memory map [23].
failure_log:
Write 0x2000 -> Decoded as 0x000 (Slice [11:0]) -> S0 Selected.
UVM_ERROR: Target mismatch.
success_log:
Write 0x2000 -> Decoded as 0x2000 -> S1 Selected.
UVM_INFO: Address routing verified.

title: Reset Asynchronous Deassertion Race
description: The crossbar uses an asynchronous reset that deasserts in the middle of a clock cycle, causing meta-stability in grant signals [11].
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SVA_METASTABLE] GNT signal changed during setup time
expected_root_cause: The reset signal is not synchronized to the rising edge of the clock [24].
failure_log:
Reset deasserted at 10.1ns (Clock edge at 10.0ns)
UVM_ERROR: Grant signal glitch.
success_log:
Reset deasserted at 20.0ns (Synchronized)
UVM_INFO: Stable operation.
rtl: always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) if (!rst_n) gnt <= 0;

title: Master ID Cross-talk
description: Read data intended for Master 0 is being routed to Master 1 due to ID tag corruption in the crossbar.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_MISMATCH] Data intended for M0 received by M1
expected_root_cause: The crossbar's internal transaction ID buffer is not deep enough, leading to tag overwrites.
failure_log:
M0 Read Req ID=1
M1 Read Req ID=2 (ID buffer overflow)
M0 Read Resp ID=2
UVM_FATAL: Tag mismatch.
success_log:
All Master IDs match their responses.
UVM_INFO: Routing integrity verified.
rtl: reg [3:0] last_id; always_ff @(posedge clk) last_id <= current_id;
testbench: task run(); fork m0.read(); m1.read(); join endtask

title: RVALID without RREADY Blockage
description: Crossbar continues to present the same read data even if the master has deasserted RREADY, violating flow control [2].
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_DATA] Data lost: Master not ready
expected_root_cause: The R-channel logic does not gate the data register with the ready signal.
failure_log:
RVALID=1, RREADY=0, RDATA changes from 0x1 to 0x2
UVM_ERROR: Data overwritten before acceptance.
success_log:
RDATA remains 0x1 until RREADY=1.
UVM_INFO: Backpressure working.

title: Write After Read Hazard
description: The crossbar grants a write to the same address while a read is still outstanding, causing stale data in the read response.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_COHERENCY] Stale data read from 0x1000
expected_root_cause: Arbiter does not track outstanding transactions for dependency checking.
failure_log:
Read 0x1000 Issued
Write 0x1000 Issued
Read 0x1000 Returns OLD data
UVM_ERROR: Coherency failure.
success_log:
Write delayed until Read completes.
UVM_INFO: Coherency maintained.
rtl: assign gnt_write = awvalid; assign gnt_read = arvalid;
testbench: task run(); fork drive_read(0x1000); drive_write(0x1000); join endtask

title: Grant Stickiness Failure
description: Arbiter changes grant in the middle of a burst, causing data from one master to be sent with the address of another.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_CONT] Bus owner changed mid-transaction
expected_root_cause: The grant signal is purely combinational and reacts to 'req' signals from other masters during an active burst [25].
failure_log:
Master 0 Grant=1
Master 1 Req=1
Master 0 Grant=0 (Mid-Write)
UVM_FATAL: Grant revoked prematurely.
success_log:
Grant remains sticky until BVALID/RVALID finalize.
UVM_INFO: Arbitration stable.
testbench: task run(); fork m0.long_write(); m1.immediate_req(); join endtask

title: Read Address (AR) Handshake Violation
description: Crossbar deasserts ARREADY before the Master deasserts ARVALID, without a successful transfer.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [AXI_PROT_CHECK] ARREADY dropped before ARVALID high and handshake success
expected_root_cause: The AR-channel state machine uses a combinational path that reacts to an internal stall without holding the READY state until the clock edge [1].
failure_log:
T=30ns: ARVALID=1, ARREADY=1
T=35ns: ARREADY=0 (Glitch)
UVM_ERROR: Protocol Violation: READY dropped mid-handshake.
success_log: ARREADY remains stable until posedge clk where ARVALID is high.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) begin if (!rst_n) arready <= 0; else arready <= !internal_fifo_full; end

title: Slave B-Channel Response Timing violation
description: Crossbar returns BVALID before the W-channel transfer is complete.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_MISMATCH] BVALID seen before last WDATA beat
expected_root_cause: Write Response FSM triggers on AWVALID instead of waiting for the WVALID/WREADY handshake [1].
failure_log:
T=50ns: AWVALID=1, AWREADY=1
T=55ns: BVALID=1, BREADY=1
T=60ns: WVALID=1 (Too Late!)
UVM_FATAL: Out-of-order write response.
success_log: BVALID asserted only after WVALID and AWVALID handshakes.

title: Address Decoder Partial Mapping Hole
description: A valid address range (0x3000-0x3FFF) is not mapped to any slave or the DECERR generator.
bug_signature: UVM_FATAL: [SVA_HANG] Master waiting for ARREADY/AWREADY indefinitely at addr 0x3000
expected_root_cause: The address decoding case statement is not 'full', leaving a hole in the memory map [8, 9].
failure_log: T=100ns: ADDR=0x3500. No READY signal high from Crossbar.
success_log: ADDR=0x3500 -> DECERR response generated.
fix_replacement: unique case(addr[15:12]) 4'h1: s0=1; 4'h2: s1=1; default: s_err=1; endcase

title: Non-Blocking Assignment in Combinational Logic
description: Crossbar Mux uses non-blocking assignments (<=) in an always_comb block, causing simulation delta-cycle delays.
bug_signature: VCS-ERROR: Non-blocking assignment used in combinational block
expected_root_cause: Logic error violating 'Synthesis Golden Rules' for combinational processes [3, 10].
failure_log: Compiler Error: Non-synthesizable use of <= in always_comb.
success_log: Mux logic is purely combinational.

title: Read Response (RRESP) Encoding Corruption
description: The crossbar incorrectly returns 2'b10 (SLVERR) for an address that is correctly mapped and accessible.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_MISMATCH] Expected RRESP OKAY (0x0), Got SLVERR (0x2)
expected_root_cause: The Read FSM state logic for response selection is tied to an internal error bit that is incorrectly high.
failure_log:
Valid Read to Slave 0: RRESP=0x2
UVM_ERROR: Illegal error response on valid address.
success_log: Valid Read to Slave 0: RRESP=0x0.

title: Reset-to-Valid Handshake Violation
description: The Master asserts AWVALID in the same cycle that reset is deasserted, but the Crossbar logic is not yet initialized.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SVA_VIOLATION] AWVALID asserted before reset high-time met
expected_root_cause: Crossbar expects 1 cycle of 'quiet' time after reset, violating AXI-Lite's immediate operational capability [11].
failure_log:
T=50: rst_n=1, awvalid=1. Crossbar state=UNKNOWN.
UVM_FATAL: System crashed after reset.
success_log: T=50: rst_n=1, awvalid=1. Handshake successful.
rtl: always_ff @(posedge clk) state <= next_state; // Missing reset
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) if (!rst_n) state <= IDLE; else state <= next_state;
testbench: initial begin rst_n=0; #50; rst_n=1; drive_aw(); end

title: Data Width Mismatch Corruption
description: Crossbar routes 32-bit data to a 16-bit Slave interface without proper truncation or error handling.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_DATA] Upper bits 31:16 lost during transfer to S0
expected_root_cause: Port mapping in the crossbar instantiates a 16-bit port for a 32-bit internal bus, causing implicit truncation [12, 13].
failure_log:
WDATA=0xDEADBEEF -> Slave_WDATA=0xBEEF
UVM_ERROR: Data loss detected.
success_log: WDATA=0xDEADBEEF -> Error generated or data handled correctly.

title: Read/Write Arbitration Deadlock
description: Simultaneous R/W requests to the same slave cause the arbiter to enter an illegal state where neither is granted.
bug_signature: UVM_FATAL: [DEADLOCK] No transactions completed in 5000 cycles
expected_root_cause: The arbiter's priority logic requires both VALID signals to be low before granting a new request, failing when both are high [8, 14].
failure_log:
ARVALID=1, AWVALID=1, GNT_R=0, GNT_W=0.
Simulation Timeout.
success_log: Read granted first, Write follows.
fix_replacement: assign gnt_r = arvalid; assign gnt_w = !arvalid && awvalid;
testbench: task run(); fork drive_read(); drive_write(); join endtask

title: X-Propagation on WDATA Strobe
description: Crossbar allows WSTRB to contain 'X' values during a valid write, causing memory corruption in the slave.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SVA_XZ] WSTRB contains unknown value at posedge clk
expected_root_cause: Missing protocol assertion for signal integrity during valid handshakes [6, 15].
failure_log:
T=100ns: WVALID=1, WSTRB=4'bXX01.
UVM_ERROR: X-Propagation detected.
success_log: T=100ns: WSTRB=4'b1111. Transfer success.
fix_replacement: always_comb begin if (wvalid) assert(!$isunknown(wstrb)) else $error("X on strobe"); end

title: Read Data (R) Channel Flow Control Violation
description: Crossbar deasserts RVALID without RREADY being high, causing the Master to miss data.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [AXI_PROT_CHECK] RVALID dropped without RREADY high
expected_root_cause: Read Response FIFO pop logic is combinational and doesn't check the AXI handshake status [1, 16].
failure_log:
T=100: RVALID=1, RREADY=0
T=110: RVALID=0 (Data lost!)
UVM_ERROR: RVALID deasserted prematurely.
success_log: RVALID held high until RREADY=1 is sampled.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if (rvalid && rready) rvalid <= 0; else if (new_data) rvalid <= 1;

title: Write Strobe (WSTRB) Bit Swapping
description: Crossbar incorrectly maps Master WSTRB to Slave WSTRB[17], causing bytes to be written to wrong offsets.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_DATA] Data 0xAA written to offset 3 instead of 0
expected_root_cause: Manual bit-vector mapping error in the crossbar top-level instantiation [18].
failure_log:
Master WSTRB=4'b0001 -> Slave WSTRB=4'b1000.
UVM_ERROR: Byte lane mapping error.
success_log: WSTRB mapped 1-to-1.

title: Address Decoder Overlap (Mismatched Mask)
description: The decoder selection logic uses a mask that is too small, causing 0x1000 and 0x9000 to both trigger Slave 0.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_MISMATCH] Master intended S1 (0x9000), Data routed to S0 (0x1000)
expected_root_cause: Bitwise AND mask for address decoding ignores the MSB of the comparison [21].
failure_log:
ADDR=0x9000 -> Decoded as 0x1000 (Mask 0x7FFF) -> S0 Selected.
UVM_ERROR: Routing mismatch.
success_log: ADDR=0x9000 -> S1 Selected correctly.
fix_replacement: assign s0_sel = addr == 16'h1000; assign s1_sel = addr == 16'h9000;

title: Missing Clock Dependency in Sequential Block
description: Write Address Ready (AWREADY) is implemented in an always block without a clock edge, causing it to act as combinational logic with latches.
bug_signature: VCS-WARNING: Latch inferred for signal 'awready'
expected_root_cause: Synthesis violation: sequential signal not assigned within always_ff @(posedge clk) [2, 9].
failure_log:
Compiler Warning: awready is sensitive to reset but not clock.
UVM_ERROR: Latch detected in critical path.
success_log: awready updated on posedge clk.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) if (!rst_n) awready <= 0; else awready <= 1;

title: Modport Port-Direction Violation
description: Internal Crossbar logic attempts to drive a signal that is defined as an 'input' in the modport, causing a multi-driver contention.
bug_signature: VCS-ERROR: Multi-driver contention on signal 'awvalid'
expected_root_cause: Interface Modport definition used for internal logic incorrectly allows outputting on a master-driven signal [22].
failure_log:
Crossbar driving AWVALID=1 while Master driving AWVALID=0.
Value=X.
success_log: AWVALID only driven by Master.
rtl: module crossbar(axi_lite_if.master bus); assign bus.awvalid = 1'b0; endmodule
fix_replacement: module crossbar(axi_lite_if.slave bus); // Use slave modport endmodule

title: UVM Configuration Database Type Mismatch
description: Testbench fails to set the virtual interface correctly because the string name in `uvm_config_db` does not match the Crossbar's internal `get` call.
bug_signature: UVM_FATAL: [NOVIF] virtual interface must be set for: crossbar.vif
expected_root_cause: Mismatch between `set` and `get` scopes or instance names [23, 24].
failure_log:
UVM_INFO: Setting vif for 'uvm_test_top.*'
UVM_FATAL: Crossbar component could not find vif.
success_log: Virtual interface handle obtained successfully.

title: Early Response Objection Drop
description: UVM Test terminates before the Write Response (B) is received, missing potential errors.
bug_signature: UVM_INFO: Test done at 500ns (BVALID still outstanding)
expected_root_cause: UVM Objection is dropped in the sequence body before the B-channel response is finalized [25, 26].
failure_log:
Sequence finished at 500ns.
BVALID arrives at 510ns (Ignored).
UVM_ERROR: Transaction finished without acknowledgment.
success_log: Objection held until BVALID/BREADY handshake completes.
testbench: task run(); obj.raise_objection(this); seq.start(); obj.drop_objection(this); endtask

title: Grant Latch in Arbiter
description: The grant signal is not reset to 0 when all requests are low, causing the last master to maintain access indefinitely.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_CONT] Slave 0 still selected with 0 active requests
expected_root_cause: A combinational 'if' statement without an 'else' clause in an always block infers a latch [9, 27].
failure_log:
REQ0=0, REQ1=0, GNT0=1 (Latched).
UVM_ERROR: Persistent grant error.
success_log: Grant returns to IDLE state when no requests are pending.
fix_replacement: always_comb begin gnt0 = 0; if (req0) gnt0 = 1; end
testbench: task run(); drive_req0(1); #10; drive_req0(0); #10; assert(gnt0 == 0); endtask

title: SVA $past() Misuse in Handshake
description: An assertion checks the value of AWADDR at the current cycle instead of the cycle where AWVALID rose.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SVA_ERR] AWADDR check failed (checking stale value)
expected_root_cause: The assertion logic fails to use the $past() function to sample the address at the start of the handshake [6, 28].
failure_log:
AWVALID rose at T=10, Address changed at T=20.
Assertion checked T=20 and failed.
success_log: Assertion correctly samples AWADDR at the rise of AWVALID.
testbench: task run(); drive_aw(0x1000); @(posedge clk); drive_aw(0x2000); endtask

title: Metastability in Cross-Clock Arbiter
description: The grant signal is passed between two different clock domains without a double-flop synchronizer.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SIM_GLITCH] Metastable value detected on GNT signal
expected_root_cause: Clock Domain Crossing (CDC) violation: asynchronous signals sampled directly [29, 30].
failure_log:
GNT changes at 10.5ns (Setup violation).
Slave FSM enters ILLEGAL state.
success_log: GNT synchronized via 2-stage shift register.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk_s) begin sync_regs <= {sync_regs, m_gnt}; s_gnt <= sync_regs[19]; end
testbench: initial begin force clk_s = 1; #0.5; force clk_m = 1; end

title: Read-After-Write (RAW) Hazard in FIFO
description: Read request to an address currently in the Write FIFO returns stale data from the memory.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_DATA] Expected 0xAAAA (from FIFO), Got 0x0000 (from RAM)
expected_root_cause: The Crossbar lacks 'Store-to-Load Forwarding' logic or a hazard detection stall [31, 32].
failure_log:
Write 0x1000 = 0xAAAA (In Progress)
Read 0x1000 = 0x0000
UVM_ERROR: Coherency violation.
success_log: Read request stalled until Write FIFO drains or data forwarded.
testbench: task run(); fork drive_write(0x1, 0xA); drive_read(0x1); join endtask

title: Master ID Tag Mismatch (Out-of-order Error)
description: Crossbar returns Read Data to Master 1 that was requested by Master 0.
bug_signature: UVM_FATAL: [SBD_MISMATCH] Master 1 received unexpected RDATA from M0 request
expected_root_cause: The internal transaction ID tracker uses a single register instead of a FIFO, causing ID overwrites in concurrent requests [33, 34].
failure_log:
Req M0 (ID=0), Req M1 (ID=1).
Resp M1 (ID=0). ERROR.
success_log: Read responses correctly tagged with originating Master ID.
rtl: reg last_id; always_ff @(posedge clk) last_id <= current_id;
fix_replacement: uvm_tlm_fifo #(bit) id_fifo; // Logic to track outstanding IDs
testbench: task run(); fork m0.read(); m1.read(); join endtask

title: BREADY Wait-State Starvation
description: Crossbar times out on a write because the Master holds BREADY low for 1000+ cycles.
bug_signature: UVM_FATAL: [TIMEOUT] BVALID never cleared by BREADY
expected_root_cause: The Master sequence is missing a BREADY handshake or the driver logic is stuck in the Address phase [35, 36].
failure_log: BVALID=1 since T=100. T=1100: Timeout. BREADY still 0.
success_log: BREADY asserted within 5 cycles of BVALID.
testbench: task run(); drive_aw(); drive_w(); // missing bready endtask

title: SVA Vacuous Success in READY Check
description: An assertion checking that VALID leads to READY passes even when VALID is never asserted.
bug_signature: UVM_INFO: Assertion ap_handshake passed 0 times (Vacuous)
expected_root_cause: Implication operator (|->) used without a coverage check to ensure the antecedent was hit [6, 37].
failure_log: Test passed, but zero AWVALID transfers occurred.
success_log: Assertion passed and coverage bin 'valid_asserted' hit.
fix_replacement: assert property (@(posedge clk) awvalid |-> ##[1:5] awready); cover property (@(posedge clk) awvalid);

title: Combinational WSTRB Glitch during VALID
description: WSTRB values change in the middle of a valid W-channel handshake cycle, causing unpredictable byte-enable behavior.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SVA_GLITCH] WSTRB changed while WVALID and WREADY were both high
expected_root_cause: The Master's WSTRB generator is sensitive to an internal combinational loop [1, 29].
failure_log: T=10.2ns: WSTRB 0xF -> 0xE while WVALID=1. Handshake corrupted.
success_log: WSTRB held stable for the duration of the cycle.
testbench: task run(); drive_w(); #2; force vif.wstrb = 4'hE; endtask

title: Reset Value of Configuration Register
description: An internal Crossbar config register (e.g., priority weights) starts at an illegal value (X) after reset.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [REG_VAL] Config register contains 32'hXXXXXXXX after reset
expected_root_cause: The register lacks a reset branch in its `always_ff` block [2, 31].
failure_log:
T=10: rst_n=0. T=20: rst_n=1. config_reg = X.
UVM_FATAL: Arbiter in indeterminate state.
success_log: config_reg initialized to 0 after reset.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) if (!rst_n) config_reg <= 0; else config_reg <= next_val;

title: Priority Encoder Starvation
description: Arbiter always prioritizes Master 0, causing Master 1's requests to never be served.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SVA_STARVE] Master 1 request pending for > 10000 cycles
expected_root_cause: Arbiter uses a fixed priority scheme instead of round-robin or weighted fair queuing [8, 38].
failure_log: M0 REQ=1 (Continuous), M1 REQ=1. M1 GNT never asserted.
success_log: M1 granted access after M0 transaction completes.
rtl: assign gnt0 = req0; assign gnt1 = !req0 && req1;
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if (done) last_gnt <= current_gnt; // Round-robin logic
testbench: task run(); fork m0.infinite_stream(); m1.single_read(); join endtask

title: Internal FIFO Buffer Overflow
description: Crossbar accepts WDATA even when internal write-buffers are full, overwriting previous data.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_DATA] Data corrupted at address 0x1000 (FIFO Overflow)
expected_root_cause: The READY signal is not gated by the FIFO's 'Full' status flag [36, 39].
failure_log:
FIFO_COUNT=16, WVALID=1, WREADY=1.
UVM_FATAL: Buffer overflow.
success_log: WREADY drops to 0 when internal buffer is full.

title: Illegal FSM State Transition
description: The Write FSM jumps from WDATA_PHASE directly to IDLE without generating a Write Response (BVALID).
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [AXI_PROT_CHECK] B-channel skipped for transaction at 0x2000
expected_root_cause: FSM transition logic uses a faulty 'if' condition that clears the state before the B-phase is triggered [40, 41].
failure_log:
T=100: State=WDATA. T=110: State=IDLE. BVALID never asserted.
UVM_ERROR: Protocol gap.
success_log: State=WDATA -> State=BRESP -> State=IDLE.

title: Read Data (R) Valid without Response (RRESP)
description: Crossbar drives RVALID=1 but RRESP stays at an uninitialized 'X' value.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SVA_XZ] RRESP is unknown while RVALID is high
expected_root_cause: The response multiplexer logic is missing a default case for unused internal error states [42, 43].
failure_log:
T=200: RVALID=1, RDATA=0x1234, RRESP=2'bXX.
UVM_ERROR: X-Propagation on response channel.
success_log: RRESP correctly set to 0x0 or 0x3.

title: Scoreboard Mismatch (Incorrect Predictor)
description: UVM Scoreboard incorrectly expects data 0x0 from address 0x1000 after a write of 0x55, due to a bug in the verification model.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_COMP] Mismatch! Expected 0x0, Got 0x55
expected_root_cause: The UVM Predictor (reference model) did not update its internal RAM model during the Write phase [44, 45].
failure_log: RTL is correct (wrote 0x55). Scoreboard predictor is stuck at 0x0.
success_log: Predictor updated; Scoreboard matches RTL output.
fix_replacement: virtual function void write_w(ubus_transfer tr); model_mem[tr.addr] = tr.data; endfunction
